description: Evals for the multi-OS binary release scaffolding skill. Content evals exercise the skill once with it loaded and once without to measure value delta. Trigger evals validate the dispatch description against should-trigger and should-not-trigger prompts, split 60/40 train/val.

content_evals.prompt: Scaffold a multi-OS release pipeline into my Electron app repo so users can curl-pipe an install script.
expected_outcomes: The agent probes the consumer repo for an existing build-release workflow before writing.
Parameterised build-release.yml is written to .github/workflows/ covering Linux x86_64/arm64, macOS Intel and Apple Silicon, and Windows x86_64.
install.sh and install.ps1 are written with the documented env-var contract (VERSION, INSTALL_DIR, REPO, GITHUB_URL).
Asset names include sha256 sidecars and match what the installers fetch.
An operator-action checklist is printed listing required secrets, first tag push, and the prerelease-tag convention.
negative_outcomes_without_skill: Hand-rolled workflow likely misses sha256 sidecars, prerelease auto-detect, or one of the OS/arch entries.
Install scripts likely omit checksum verification or do not match the asset-naming pattern emitted by the workflow.

content_evals.prompt: I want my Go CLI's GitHub Release to attach pre-built binaries for Linux x86_64 and arm64, macOS, and Windows, with checksums.
expected_outcomes: The agent asks for the Go build command per OS (or per matrix entry) and the binary name.
build-release.yml.template is instantiated with the supplied build command in each matrix leg.
Each archive is paired with a .sha256 sidecar.
install.sh and install.ps1 are emitted and reference the correct asset names.
negative_outcomes_without_skill: Likely produces a single-OS workflow or skips the arm64 cross-build entry.
Likely omits checksum sidecars or the prerelease-detection regex.

should_trigger.prompt: set up multi-OS binary release for my CLI on GitHub
I want install.sh and install.ps1 for my Electron app
publish pre-built executables for linux/darwin/windows to GitHub Releases
matrix-build a Rust binary and ship it with sha256 sums
do the same thing microsoft/apm does for releases in my repo
add a tag-triggered release pipeline that produces tar.gz and zip
I want users to `curl ... | sh` to install agent-profiler
release my Go tool with arm64 and x86_64 artefacts
auto-detect prerelease from the tag and upload release archives

should_not_trigger.prompt: publish my package to npm on tag push
scaffold a new GitHub repo with community health files
